commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes gateway post-attach startup so provider-auth prewarming only schedules when `providerAuthPrewarm.enabled` is true and adds a regression test for the omitted-enabled path.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +20. Total +20 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. full live CPU reproduction was run here, but the default scheduling path is source-reproducible: current main and `v2026.5.27` pass `providerAuthPrewarm` without `enabled`, and the guard treats that as enabled. The contributor supplied after-fix live startup proof for the PR head.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A focused repair can split auth invalidation from warm scheduling and fix the stale test assertion; maintainers still own the final shipped-default decision before merge.

target.title: fix(gateway): disable provider auth prewarm by default
target.body:
What does this PR do?
- Stops the gateway startup path from enabling provider-auth prewarming unless it is explicitly opted in.
- Adds a regression test that verifies the startup path does not schedule the prewarm sidecar by default.

Root cause
- The post-attach startup flow treated `providerAuthPrewarm` as enabled unless callers explicitly set `enabled: false`, so the normal gateway boot path always scheduled the prewarm work.

Fix
- Require `providerAuthPrewarm.enabled === true` before scheduling provider-auth prewarming.
- Add a regression test that passes the startup config without `enabled: true` and verifies no prewarm sidecar is scheduled.

Why this shape
- Keeps the prewarm hook available for explicit opt-in flows.
- Makes the default gateway boot path lighter and avoids startup CPU spikes.

Tests
- `/opt/homebrew/bin/node ./node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`

Related PRs / issues
- Fixes #86512
target.author: wesleysimplicio

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
